spec_match.rationale: Accurately compares the multi-step event-level source registration chain against first-party partitioned cookie tracking.

interactive.rationale: Provides a responsive 'Run probe' button that runs browser checks, updates table badges, and populates a summary status block.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligned perfectly with parent description: shows old event-level 5-step chain against recommended server-side replacements with capability probes.

edge_cases.rationale: Features error-catching around Feature Policy tests and notes JS detection limits (e.g. server-side headers require network hops).

references.rationale: Directly links to four related local concept pages and the main feature index.

design_system.rationale: Conforms nicely to colors, table grid layout, crumb links, and code highlighting styles.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Fresh Chrome DevTools MCP v152 sweep loaded the copied concept, exercised every detected control, found named controls, and reported no post-triage runtime/action failure.

conformance.rationale: A v152 suite now carries the same feature-ID/spec contract without modifying the immutable source-milestone suite.

summary: A clear, highly instructive educational resource contrasting old client-heavy ARA flows with standard, robust server-side conversion logging. Fully interactive with live diagnostics, try-catch safety structures, and exceptional alignment with design guidelines.
